title: WIP: Cirrus: Add Ubuntu to rootless integration testing

comments.body:
LGTM
I like more tests.
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Sadly test are breaking.

> Sadly test are breaking.

That's a matter of perspective; One *could* say the tests have freshly uncovered previously hidden problems :grin: 

* [int podman ubuntu-2004 rootless host](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6259580062662656/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2004-rootless-host.log.html)
* [int podman ubuntu-2010 rootless host](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5133680155820032/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2010-rootless-host.log.html)

Let's see what we got going on here...

...okay, these appear like fairly clear problems:

* For Ubuntu 20.10 (crun), [only one test failed with a pretty clear error](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5133680155820032/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2010-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-play-kube-allows-setting-resource-limits--2): `error starting container a7a...6c2: the requested cgroup controller `cpu` is not available: OCI runtime error`.  Assuming that means some thing like "The kernel is too old", maybe this is a test we can simply skip on Ubuntu 20.10?
* For Ubuntu 20.04 (runc), [all the errors are capability related](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6259580062662656/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2004-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-network-prune--2), so again perhaps a "kernel is too old" issue.  @rhatdan what do we do in case of `Error: OCI runtime error: container_linux.go:367: starting container process caused: unknown capability "CAP_PERFMON"`?

The `Error: OCI runtime error: container_linux.go:367: starting container process caused: unknown capability "CAP_PERFMON"` error only happens in network tests. I completely rework rootless networking in #9423. Maybe it will work with the new networking logic. I added this commit to my PR lets see if it will work there.
@Luap99 thanks!  I also have two PRs to update the VM images, both are stuck waiting for an updated `crun`, which could (maybe) have some bearing here, esp. on the first (Ubuntu 20.10) problem.
Ubuntu 20.04 works with my PR https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9423/checks?check_run_id=2238965171.
New map: ![cirrus-map-pr9905](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/113208989-b56efc80-922f-11eb-92b5-d0aeb6a4c0a4.png)

@Luap99 excellent news, does it also pass standalone and/or do you want me to rebase this?

@giuseppe or @rhatdan any ideas on the `error starting container a7a...6c2: the requested cgroup controller 'cpu' is not available: OCI runtime error`?

@cevich I think the fix for the cpu cgroup controller failure is just to skip the test, see:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/5798102679cc6e7def72c33f1a6ec0782ff195ee/test/e2e/play_kube_test.go#L1830-L1833
However the `SkipIfUnprivilegedCPULimits` only skips when rootless on fedora
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/5798102679cc6e7def72c33f1a6ec0782ff195ee/test/e2e/common_test.go#L608-L613

So I guess we have to add ubuntu 20.10 there as well.

> So I guess we have to add ubuntu 20.10 there as well.

Ahh perfect.  @giuseppe or @rhatdan un-ping, and sorry for the noise.  Let's see if we can just remove `&& info.Distribution == "fedora"`...
Rebased onto #9423 then again onto master.
Rebased on master
Rebased on master
Blocked by https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9997
Rebased on master
Obsolete by #10237

@cevich: PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
